items.briefing:
Australia's SMS Sender ID Register launched today, requiring businesses to register their SMS sender names to help block scammers impersonating trusted brands. This means Australians should soon see fewer fake texts pretending to be from banks or government agencies. Separately, research warns that Asian firms — including Australian organisations — are adopting AI tools faster than they're building resilience around them, raising concerns about over-reliance and security gaps.

UpGuard has also flagged that pirate football streaming sites are linked to offshore betting operations, posing real risks to Australians who use them. **Practical tip:** Avoid unofficial streaming sites for sport — they may expose your device to malware or harvest your personal data.

featured.title: SMS Sender ID Register goes live to help protect Australians from scams
featured.quote: Australia's new SMS Sender ID Register directly protects citizens from prevalent scam threats.
